english: Red Bull reduced sugar energy drink

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'energy drink' class (FOODON_03301589); no specific Red Bull Less Sugar variant exists. Closest parent match applied — the general energy drink class is the nearest defensible ancestor in the FoodOn hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code for commercial energy drink product; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 250 ml can of Red Bull Less Sugar, equivalent to approximately 260 g, is the typical single-serve portion consumed as an energy drink.

nutrient_source.title: Red Bull Energy Drink (Less Sugar) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Red Bull (less sugar), cr_id D05020012, with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat reported as 0.0 g by formulation (no fat present). Monounsaturated, polyunsaturated, and trans fat set to -1 as HPB does not report fatty acid fractions for this product. Potassium, cholesterol, vitamins, and other micronutrients set to -1 where HPB does not report them for this entry.

gi_source.title: Glucose drinks and sugar-containing carbonated beverages — GI values from the University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate applied to Red Bull Less Sugar based on sucrose/glucose solution of equivalent concentration
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Red Bull Less Sugar exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 65 (medium) is estimated by analogy to sucrose-glucose carbonated beverages of similar sugar concentration (~12 g/100 ml) documented in the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI x carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 x 30.7) / 100 = 20.0. Carbohydrate per 250 ml serving = 12.28 g/100g x 2.5 = 30.7 g. Note: input record stated GL 8.0 which appears to be per 100 g basis; corrected GL per 250 ml serving = 20.0. Input GI confidence was 0.357 (low); analogous estimate from approved source applied and documented.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (12.28 g/100 ml) classifying this as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB), combined with caffeine-mediated acute blood pressure elevation
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid Red Bull Less Sugar: the 12.28 g/100 ml sugar load independently raises blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 SSB guidelines, and caffeine causes acute BP elevation; only the zero-sugar variant with caffeine monitoring is potentially acceptable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage (12.28 g sugar/100 ml) with zero dietary fibre, causing rapid postprandial glucose spike; classified as SSB per ADA guidelines
type2_diabetes.guidance: Red Bull Less Sugar is unsuitable for type 2 diabetes management: 12.28 g sugar per 100 ml in liquid form with no fibre buffer causes rapid glucose absorption and significant postprandial hyperglycaemia; the 'less sugar' label does not confer clinical acceptability and the ADA classifies this as an SSB to be avoided.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sugar load as SSB linked to dyslipidaemia and increased CVD risk; caffeine and taurine combination may affect cardiac rhythm in susceptible individuals
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this product: regular SSB consumption at this sugar concentration is associated with elevated triglycerides, reduced HDL, and increased CVD event risk per AHA dietary guidelines, and the caffeine-taurine combination warrants caution in those with arrhythmia or heart failure.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pro-inflammatory sugar load (12.28 g/100 ml) with zero fibre and no anti-inflammatory components; caffeine may interact with methotrexate
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Red Bull Less Sugar is unsuitable for rheumatoid arthritis management: the sugar load promotes inflammatory mediators, no anti-inflammatory nutrients are present, and caffeine may interact with methotrexate and other RA medications.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Red Bull Energy Drink (Less Sugar) is a commercially produced, carbonated, ready-to-drink energy beverage manufactured by Red Bull GmbH (Austria) and widely distributed globally including Singapore. It is formulated with carbonated water, sucrose, glucose, citric acid, taurine (an amino acid), caffeine (approximately 32 mg per 100 ml), B-vitamins (niacin, pantothenic acid, B6, B12), and flavourings. The 'less sugar' variant contains approximately 12.28 g of sugar per 100 ml, which is reduced compared to the original Red Bull. The drink is lightly sweet, mildly tart from citric acid, and has a characteristic herbal-medicinal flavour profile from taurine and B-vitamins. It is consumed chilled, typically from a 250 ml aluminium can. Nutritionally, it provides 50 kcal per 100 ml, 0.4 g protein, 0 g fat (confirmed zero by formulation), and 12.28 g carbohydrate (all as sugar). Dietary fibre is absent. Sodium is 42 mg per 100 ml. Despite the 'less sugar' marketing claim, this product remains a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) per ADA and AHA classification thresholds, and is clinically unsuitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The caffeine content contributes to acute blood pressure elevation and may interact with medications including methotrexate used in rheumatoid arthritis. The glycemic index is estimated at 65 (medium) by analogy to sucrose-glucose carbonated beverages of equivalent concentration, with a glycemic load of approximately 20.0 per 250 ml serving. This product is ultra-processed and classified under the Beverages / Electrolyte-Energy drinks subgroup in the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D05020012).

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D05020012) with confidence 0.95. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database applied to a sucrose-glucose carbonated beverage of equivalent sugar concentration; confidence is medium for GI specifically. Overall record confidence is high given exact HPB source match for all core macros and secondary nutrients.
notes: fat_g is confirmed zero by product formulation — Red Bull Less Sugar contains no fat by design (confirmed per HPB Foodo entry D05020012 and consistent with Red Bull product label). saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero for the same reason. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero — this is a liquid carbonated beverage with no fibre-containing ingredients. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: HPB Foodo does not report fatty acid fractions for this product. potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: HPB Foodo does not report these micronutrient values for this specific product entry; detailed micronutrient panel not available in HPB or USDA Branded Foods for this exact SKU as of 2026-04-16. Note: Red Bull does contain B-vitamins (niacin, B6, B12, pantothenic acid) by formulation but exact per-100g values are not available in the approved HPB source; these are set to -1 pending label-verified data from an approved source. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate; original input GI confidence was 0.357 (low). Glycemic load per 250 ml serving corrected to 20.0 (input stated 8.0 which appears to have been calculated on a per-100g basis rather than per serving). The 'less sugar' label does not confer clinical acceptability for SSB-sensitive conditions. Halal status not confirmed from approved source; set to false pending certification verification. Iteration 3 fix: ontology.foodon_match_type corrected from 'broad' to 'closest_parent' to conform to the allowed values ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none'] as required by the validator.

Iteration 2: Recalculated glycemic_load_per_serving to per-serving basis (20.0 for 250ml can). Verified alias consistency between flat aliases and aliases_by_language.
